global_strategy: PRIMARY OBJECTIVE:
Win the game as quickly as possible by destroying the enemy base.
Resource collection, unit production, and defense are only valuable if they lead to faster victory or prevent immediate loss.
If a realistic attack opportunity exists, prefer game-ending pressure over passive optimization.
Aggressive phase transition:
Harvest immediately in the early game and stabilize minimum economy first.
Build one barracks as soon as resources allow.
After a barracks exists, shift priority from additional economy toward sustained military production.
Once at least 2 combat units are available, begin attacking instead of waiting for a perfect army.
Do not stall for ideal conditions:
Do not keep accumulating resources without spending them when useful production or pressure is available.
Do not delay attacks only to build a larger army.
Do not overproduce workers once resource income is stable for current production needs.
Base-kill orientation:
If the enemy base location is known, prioritize plans that create direct pressure on the enemy base.
Enemy production structures and economy are more valuable targets than random distant skirmishes.
Convert local military advantage into forward pressure instead of prolonged waiting.

At every timestep decide actions using the following priority order.
